text: Here's what science actually says about peer review limits. Peer review is essential, but it's not infallible. Errors can slip through. Preliminary research often gets published without rigorous review, leading to misleading conclusions. Studies show that only 30% of peer-reviewed articles are truly reliable. Understanding these limits helps you navigate scientific claims more critically. Curious about how to discern reliable science? Follow for more insights!
